governing.notesMd: Pending decisions are non-governing until confirmed, but they gate implementation where indicated.

items.title: Confirm exact Clear Filters confirmation copy

decision.question: Should the Clear Filters confirmation copy explicitly say that active Jobs results will broaden after confirmation?
decision.whyItMatters: Exact copy may determine whether users understand a broad reset before committing it.
decision.decisionNeededBy: Before marking desired Clear Filters behavior as confirmed governing.

options.summary: Use explicit confirmation copy for broad reset behavior.
options.tradeoffsMd: Reduces surprise and improves proof clarity, but adds interruption to the clear path.

options.summary: Use the existing Clear Filters label without confirmation copy.
options.tradeoffsMd: Keeps the interaction lighter, but increases risk that users do not understand the reset scope.

consequencesIfUnresolved: Clear Filters desired behavior cannot become confirmed governing.
Implementation should not ship the behavior change as complete.

recommendation.summary: Use explicit confirmation copy for broad reset behavior.
recommendation.rationaleMd: Clear/reset behavior can be destructive to browsing context, so copy should reduce surprise.

alternatives.summary: Use the existing Clear Filters label without confirmation copy.
alternatives.tradeoffsMd: Less interruption, but higher risk of accidental context loss.

recommendation.consequencesMd: If accepted, exact confirmation copy should become governing for Clear Filters.

confirmation.notesMd: Confirmation required before implementation can ship changed broad reset behavior.

confirmationBaseline.notesMd: Backfilled as the audit baseline for the one-pass UX spec creation/update. This records provenance only; it does not replace UX authority, product confirmation, behavior mappings, or runtime drift ledgers.
